description: Figure 14 presents finite element simulation results showing yield strength distribution maps (state variable SDV5, in MPa) for Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel during warm multiaxial forging (MAF). The color contour scale ranges from approximately 699 MPa (dark blue) to 1358 MPa (red). Three pairs of cross-sectional views, each labeled with distinct X-Y-Z coordinate orientations, display the progressive evolution of yield strength across successive MAF passes. From left to right, the overall yield strength levels increase markedly: the leftmost pair shows predominantly green and blue regions indicating lower strength, the middle pair exhibits yellow and orange zones of intermediate strength, and the rightmost pair reveals extensive red and orange cores signifying the highest strength. In all views, the strength is maximum at the geometric center and decreases toward the surfaces. These maps correspond to simulations integrating a modified Johnson-Cook constitutive model with strain-dependent dislocation density and grain size parameters, visually confirming that cumulative plastic strain during MAF progressively raises yield strength through the combined effects of dislocation strengthening and grain refinement.
